highlights.text: Heroes Awakening page. A brand-new, complete guide to the new hero system, now front and center on the main wheel. Everything you need to understand Hyper Awakening, Hero Swap, and Hero Skills lives in one place.

Three systems, never mixed up. Hyper Awakening, Hero Swap, and Hero Skills are kept clearly separate so you always know which path you are looking at, with Fight Power and Combat Power totals corrected.
Per-skill level slider. Drag through all 10 levels of any skill and watch the resolved skill text and its actual values update at every tick, so you can see exactly what each level buys you.
Skill detail. Tap a skill for a clean, fully readable description. You see what the skill does in plain words, never a wall of raw numbers.
What-if calculator. Pick a target level for a skill and instantly see the resolved text plus the cost to get there, so you can plan before you spend.
Tier Upgrade Costs tab. A full Awakening Stone ladder broken down per tier, with an all-four-skills-to-Tier-6 milestone card so you know the total bill at a glance.
Awakening Stones calculator. Plan a 40-level run with a complete cost ladder, plus the rules for Awakening Time and the Hero Grand Slam event laid out clearly.
Hero Swap, organized for you. Swaps are grouped by army type with combo-trio groupings (P1 to P2 to P3) so the right pairings are easy to spot.
Real Cost on Grand Slam packs. The USD and JPY breakdown modal now covers Grand Slam packs, so you can see what a pack actually costs before you buy.
Whale check. A clear verdict and advice callout flags what is whale-only, so if you are not chasing the top spend you know exactly what to skip.
Awakened heroes in your Armory. Awakened portraits and a diamond badge now show up across your Armory Report, on the Overview, in Combat Heroes, and on the Bench.
Awakened skills everywhere. Awakened skills now render in your Battle Report and in the Bench detail view, so your full setup reads correctly.
Rune Pool tab. See every unequipped rune plus the 0-star runes you can pull off idle gold gear, all in one place. No more guessing what you have to work with.

Rune Advisor. Share a link with a teammate so they can build you a step-by-step plan (place, merge, inherit, recycle). You pick the steps you want, accept them, and your rune pool updates. Each merge step shows the rune's current effect and what it becomes after the upgrade, so the plan reads like a recipe, not a riddle.

Hero gear gallery. The advisor sees all your heroes' titan gear in a tidy grid with the slot icon, current rune, and star bar. Click any slot to compose a step. Heroes that share a march are grouped together with the march name shown.
Recover all. One click to queue every recoverable rune for recycling at once, so your bag fills back up in seconds instead of clicking through six slots per hero.

Settings, Advisor tab. Verified S2864 members get a new tab listing every advisor request you've created and every advisor link you've opened, with status pills and cleanup controls. Open requests auto-expire after 7 days, saved plans stick around for 14.
Activity dot. Small red dot on the settings gear when something changes on your advisor requests since you last checked.
Import TopWar Data. One bookmarklet button now captures your inventory, bench beasts, gear, runes, and chips in a single pass. The per-tab Update buttons retire.
Combat Heroes tab. The old Heroes tab is now Combat Heroes, so it is clear at a glance these are the heroes pulled from your battle reports.

Bench sub-tab. See your whole hero collection, not just the ones that showed up in a battle. Owned and Not-owned heroes are split into their own sections with a per-hero detail modal you can open for any of them.

Bench search and filters. Search by name, filter by quality, and browse hero effects just like the Heroes page, with the effects filter collapsed to the top 5 by default and a show-more toggle when you want the full list.

Real talent names and skill details. Bench heroes show talent names with their actual percentages, and your equipped exclusive skill is read straight from your active preset slot and verified before its level is labeled.
Find Skill button. Hunt down where a skill lives across all your heroes and presets in one click, so you stop guessing which hero has what.
Enigma slot requirements. Each platform slot card now spells out what it needs (rarity, element, and beast type) behind a clear Required label, with multi-type slots read correctly.
Shared reports show heroes too. When you share your report, the people you share it with now see your Combat Heroes and Bench, and the identity screen tells you whether your heroes data is loaded.
Cleaner report history. Enigma and merged reports now sort by when the report happened, show the report dates, and move the slot number where it reads more naturally.
Formation Tech tree. Your full talent tree now lives right on the Formation tab, with every node laid out the way it looks in game.
Formation planner. Plan out talent points yourself, all in your browser, so you can see exactly where a build lands before you spend anything in game.
Node detail modals. Tap any Formation Tech node to see its real name and what it does, instead of staring at raw codes.
Per-row buff breakdown. A shared buff modal opens from each row so you can read the totals each part of your formation is giving you.
Formation icons. The Formation tab now shows proper icons, so the whole panel reads at a glance.
Cloud sync. Your formation now syncs to the cloud, with a sync pill on the Formation tab so you always know your latest setup is saved. Your formation status also shows in every status indicator now.
Fixed: switchable talents like Evolved Shark were showing the wrong counts. They now read correctly.
Fixed: the Specialization +1 boost now caps at Lv 5 per slot the way it should, and node detail now shows the right numpad slots instead of raw codes.
Beast Optimizer (beta). A new sub-tab that looks at your Enigma Beasts and suggests where to place them so your field buffs come out as high as possible.
Per-swap reasoning. Every suggested move shows up as its own card with plain-language reasoning, so you can see why the optimizer wants to put a beast in a given slot before you do anything.
Condition warnings. If a suggested swap would break an active field condition you are relying on, the card flags it up front so you do not lose a buff by accident.
Swap impact in the detail view. Open a beast and see a swap-impact section that breaks down the change buff by buff, with a toggle to compare before and after.
Breeding priorities. A dedicated panel tells you which beasts are worth breeding next based on how much they would lift your buffs.
Per-unit focus. Pick a single, dual, or triple unit focus and the optimizer weights its picks toward the units you actually march with.
Titan Gear linkage. The refinement linkage row shows which hero and stat values are feeding into a beast's contribution, so the numbers tie back to real gear.
Copy as checklist. One button exports the recommended placements as a checklist you can paste somewhere and work through in game.
Fixed: swap chains. The optimizer now only recommends swap chains that raise your max-out total and never leaves a slot stranded.
New decorations wired in. Thirteen new pieces from the Travel set and Commander Stories now show up in your report, and the World Tour suit is registered as a full set.
Clickable decor sets. Tap any decoration set card to open a detail modal with the buffs at every level, so you can see exactly what each upgrade step buys you.
Not Yet Collected sets. The Decoration tab now has its own section listing the sets you do not have yet, so you know what is still out there to chase.
Smarter decor stats. The modal shows whether you own a piece and the real max level for that group, with explicit per-level buffs for the decorations that do not scale evenly.
New base skins. Five fresh skins are now visible (Mobile Bastion, Skywing, Mirage Isle, Green Frontline, and Blossom Bastion), plus sixteen older skins that were previously missing from the page.

Not Owned skins. Bases > Skins now has a Not Owned section so you can browse what you have not unlocked yet, with junk and unreleased entries filtered out. Owning a higher level of a skin hides the lower level you have outgrown.

Skin detail modals. Click any skin card to open a modal that walks through its level progression. Alternate-form skins are grouped as mutually exclusive Alternate Forms with clear Form A and Form B labels.
Whale-tier shine. When you own the top-tier Mobile Bastion skin it gets a shimmering border and pins to the top of your skins list. The Energy Circle skin is now wired in too.

One-pass capture. The Import TopWar Data bookmarklet now grabs your enigma platform layout, decorations, base skin, and formation presets all in a single run, so you do not have to capture each piece separately.
Works without a battle report. Your heroes and decorations are now built straight from your imported data, so every section of your report fills in even when you have no battle report to share.
Titan Gear Pool fallback. When there is no battle report, the advisor and report pull your heroes and gear from your saved Titan Gear Pool instead, so your build still shows up.
Clipboard-first paste. Importing now reads straight from your clipboard, which fixes long pastes getting cut off on Android keyboards.
Walkthrough videos built in. The Import window now plays short Safari and Chrome walkthroughs, so you can follow along step by step on your own phone or browser.
Cleaner beast capture. Beast extraction now keeps only your purple and gold (Q4 and up) beasts, so the data stays focused on what matters.
One bookmarklet for everything. The single Import TopWar Data bookmarklet now replaces all the old separate ones, so there is just one to keep on your bar.
March Size Checker. A new wizard on the Bench scans your bag and bench skills and tells you exactly which march-size skills to level for the biggest gain.
Lowest-link recommendations. The checker finds your weakest march-size skills and points you at the upgrades that move your march cap the most, so you stop guessing.
Per-rarity breakdown. See dual-rarity lines and a per-rarity footer, and pick the heroes you want to plan around right in the wizard.
Skill Dismantle bookmarklet. Bulk-dismantle the skill items piling up in your hero-skill bag in one pass instead of tapping through them one at a time.
Tier-split toggle. Choose whether to handle Rare and Normal skills separately or merge them, so cleanup matches how you sort.
Protect list and presets. Lock the skills you never want to touch with a protect list, and start fast with the Commonly Dismantled Skills preset.
One-tap import. Skill Dismantle is folded into the consolidated bookmarklet, so it runs alongside the rest of your imports.
Power-User Tools tab. Settings now has a Power-User Tools section, collapsed by default, that gathers the standalone bookmarklets and your Data Source Priority in one place.

Multiple advisors, one request. Two or more teammates can each build you a rune plan for the same request now, and their plans no longer overwrite each other. Settings shows a per-advisor breakdown so you can see who suggested what.
Advisor notes. Whoever builds your plan can leave a short note that shows up right above the steps, so the reasoning travels with the plan instead of getting lost in chat.

Park step plus smarter Inherit. The advisor can park a rune for later, and Inherit is now allowed onto an empty slot when another hero in that same slot is holding a rune. More of the moves you actually make in game are now plannable.
Final-state gallery. Your Advisor Plans view now shows a visual gallery of how your heroes look after the plan is applied, so you can picture the end result before committing to a single step.
Delete a plan. Advisor Plan cards now have a delete button, so old or replaced plans are easy to clear out and your list stays tidy.
On-Behalf Advising. Trusted helpers can open your report, build your rune pool and a full plan, generate a share link and report code, and ping you, all without touching any of their own saved data.

Battle Report fixes. Awakened heroes now show their proper portraits, seven heroes that were showing blank names are named (Nova, Tannis, Ella, Lophy, Sinope, Margaretha, Franziska), Navy Valhalla units show the correct attack-side and defense-side icons, and drone and formation skills are labeled correctly.
Awakening skills, now visible. Awakening skills are decoded and shown in both the Battle Report viewer and the Bench detail popup. Click one to expand it and read the full effect.

Fixed: opening a teammate's shared report code could overwrite your own saved report. Your report stays yours now. We also added missing skill icons for older heroes and tightened up loading speed and reliability across the report.

HT Chip pool — paste your unequipped chip stash and see every chip with set / slot / rarity / bound-HT / stat filters. Heavy Mothership HC-9999 gets its own tab after Blade Angel showing the 7 chips you've equipped on it.

Inventory restructured — Item tab now sorts by category. Filter chips for Skills, Titan Gear, Enigma, Gems, Formation, Units, Chests, Speed-ups, Combat Buffs, and HT Items. Pick what you're looking for instead of scrolling 1,000 things.

Gem rollup — your scattered gem packs collapse into one cell at the top showing your total. Click it for the full breakdown including gems hidden inside boxes (max-case per open). Top of the page shows direct + boxed + total potential gems.
Resource rollups — Odinium, Food, and Oil packs each collapse into one cell with K/M/B totals. Click to see every contributing pack.
Quantities >9,999 now show comma separators. Aggregates use compact K/M/B with full numbers on hover.
Enigma Beast filters — filter by Primary stat and Secondary stat (in addition to element + field) so you can hunt for beasts with the exact buffs you want.
Bug fix — Water and Wind beast elements were swapped (icon was right, label was wrong). Fixed across Enigma viewer, Battle Report, and Armory Report. Thanks Gary!

Inventory tab — your full bag, right inside your report. Item, Unit, Decor, Hero, and CPNT all in one place. Decor splits into Sets and Other so it's easier to find things. Tap anything to see what it does.
Bench Beasts — there's a new Update Bench button on the Beasts tab. Add the beasts you haven't deployed yet so the tab shows your whole collection, not just the ones in your battle reports.

Decor Upgrade Helper (beta) — click the wizard hat button on the Decoration tab. Pick the stats you want to boost (March Size, Damage, HP, etc.) and we rank the decor upgrades that give you the most boost per Universal Decor Shard. Decor pairs that don't stack are flagged, and random Lucky Chests are called out separately since you can't predict what's inside.

Unlock with your UID (S2864 only) — to upload your own bag or bench, you'll be asked for your in-game UID once as a key. We use it to check you're really an S2864 player, then forget it. Friends you share your report with can view your stuff but can't change it.
Bookmarklet help, all in one spot — the steps for setting up the bookmarklet on your phone or computer now live right inside the paste box, so you don't have to hop between pages.
Synced across your devices — your inventory and bench follow you everywhere you open your report, and they show up for anyone you share your report link with.
Polish round — around 40 missing inventory icons added, unit names cleaned up so they show levels instead of game-internal labels, popups centered properly, and you can see how fresh each upload is.
Player Report System — paste up to 6 battle report IDs to build a full account report with an animated setup wizard
One-stop-shop view: Heroes & Gear, Decorations, Base Skins, Enigma Beasts, and HT Chips all in one place
Save & Share shortcodes — generate a 4-character code to share or sync your report across devices
Sticky header with identity, language switcher, settings, and saved config management
Not Owned decoration tracker, beast bench view, hex chip layout, city skill modals, and more
Overview redesigned — compact summary chips (gear score, avg/hero, decor sets with level breakdown, beasts/HTs) and SVG donut chart for branch breakdown
New Gear Score formula — 400 refine + 600 rune per slot, normalized so maxed 1-star runes score equal to maxed 3-star runes. Click 'How is this calculated?' for the full breakdown
Top Heroes cards now show a refine/rune split bar scaled to the absolute max (6000) with '% of max' indicator
Top 3 Enigma Beasts section on Overview (sorted by deployed level)
Heroes & Gear: Group by March toggle — organize heroes into collapsible march sections with Unassigned fallback
Manage Marches: hero list now alphabetical and prevents picking the same hero in multiple slots
Decorations: DMG Boost splits into DMG Increase / Decreased DMG; Others splits into ATK / HP / Capacity
Base Decor: cards now show Equipping and Holding in Stock buff sections; Frostwoven Echo + Photonuclear Fusion display full stats
Base Decor + Base Skins: A-Z sort default with additional sort options
Decor Sets: fully-owned sets now show 'Complete' tag; unplaced components get a yellow border instead of being dimmed (set bonus is active regardless)
Roll quality colors updated to match game tiers: light blue (0-25%), purple (25-50%), yellow (50-70%), green (70%+)
Rune tooltip system — click any rune on the armory report to see its description with actual values at the current star level
Mobile tab bar no longer bounces/overscrolls sloppily
10 missing rune templateIds now resolve correctly (verified against live game TABLE)
Metric chips are now clickable — tap any chip to jump to its relevant tab (e.g., Enigma Beasts chip opens the Enigma Beasts tab)
Rune Progress by Hero — overview shows top 6 heroes with actual rune icons and star badges per gear slot, click to drill into Heroes & Gear
Rune % scaling — progress is now measured against max stars of equipped runes, so a hero with all maxed 1-star runes correctly shows 100%
Gear score breakdown tooltip — click the refine/rune progress bar on any hero card to see full Refine/Rune/Total with %-of-max
Beasts/HTs split into separate Enigma Beasts and Heavy Troopers metric cards
Decor sets level text now reads '19 Level 1, 2 Level 5' instead of 'Lv1:19'
Mobile rune layout: 2-column grid matching titan gear convention (Pistol/Backarmor/Optics left, Headset/GPS/Boots right)
'How is this calculated?' modal added for rune progress, explaining %-based scaling with a worked example
Armory report polish — march groupings now persist and sync via shortcodes, page-nav widget in the header, and a UX pass across gear cards, chips, and the overview
Battle reports entered in the armory wizard are archived to the battle data repository automatically
New Auction House / Merchant Guild reference page — listing rules, bidding, red envelopes, three-way gift shop (Auction Coin / Revenue Coin / Mega Discount), convoy restocks, and trivia
Loot Quest Collectibles catalog — 4 themes (Faces, Dinosaurs, Arms, China) covering 40 items + 4 Grail artifacts with icons
5 evolved-form base skins added, mecha icons restored on the HT Chips tab, security hardening on report collection, and general code cleanup
Storm Duel — new strategy page for the Storm Duel event, with a battlefield map, time-gated stages, lane split, squad/role assignment, silos, debuff items, and stage-keyed strategy
Storm Duel: Round 7 results — post-match Result tab with 290-player merit rankings, victory recap, and player/leader avatars
Storm Duel: Buffs tab with per-buff in-battle caps so wasted excess is surfaced clearly
Storm Duel: Google Translate language switcher in the header, with smart skips for player / alliance / leader names
Storm Duel landing card replaces the Storm's Eye beta card on the homepage
Formation V2 view added to the armory report and battle report viewer — see formations, components, talents, and Component Mastery progression
SSC Map: Round 8 data published — sector 53 post-reshuffle, 33 declarations, 8 Neutral Cities owned
SSC Map: sector power index, Neutral City capture flow, and contested-rank view added
Battle Plan rebuilt as a data-driven R8 tactical view — Recommended Focus Order, threat model, NC details, and contested fight cards written in plain English
Battle Plan: 2-Hop and 3-Hop Neutral City pathway sections with a collapsed view for the 3-hop list
Battle Plan: opponent details modal replaces the inline meta pill row
Battle Plan: buff projection now shows the value you'd get if all declarations win, plus mobile layout fixes
Enigma Beast Viewer — load any battle report to inspect beast deployments, field buffs, and activation conditions
Battle Report: Enigma Beast Comparison — side-by-side attacker vs defender beast breakdown
Guest identity: change display name + set 4-digit server number
Battle report ID passthrough between pages — no re-pasting needed
Version badge + "What's New" release notes — track site updates from the landing page
First-visit popup for major releases — auto-shows once per major version
Feedback always accessible — no identity required for anonymous submissions
Mobile layout improvements — side-by-side buttons, expanded navigation wheel
Decor Index: Base Decoration & Base Halo tabs — view castle effects and halos from battle reports
Battle Report: Base decoration comparison in decoration modal
Magic Egg Hat + 2 new Easter decorations added to index and battle report viewer
HT Chip Viewer — inspect Heavy Trooper chip loadouts, set bonuses, and random attributes from any battle report
Battle Report: HT Chips comparison button — one-click access to chip viewer for loaded reports
Storm's Eye Battle Companion — interactive map with live phase timer, building positions, and timing for the SSC Round 4 neutral city battle
One-tap Battle Companion Alerts — subscribe to push notifications 1 min before each key event (Stations, Hubs, Clusters, Boss, Burn Stage)
Storm's Eye accessible from nav wheel and Beta Features modal for identified S2864 players
Battle Report: fixed Enigma Beast field buff percentages displaying 100x too high
Battle Report + HT Chips: mixed chip sets now show dominant set with correct breakdown
Battle Report: base skin names now use correct game data (289 skins, was 129 with wrong names)
Stats Comparison: Standard, Simplified, and Group by Type filter modes — view buffs by category, folded into unit branches, or grouped by Army/Navy/Air Force
Stats Comparison: combined source drill-down — tap a simplified stat to see collapsible breakdowns per contributing buff with independent decoration drill-through
Stats Comparison: new Defense category with per-branch DEF stats (Army/Navy/Air Force) now visible
HT Chips: complete chip lookup table (268 to 354 entries) — all legendary chips across all sets now display correctly
HT Chips: set bonus calculation fixed — core chip excluded from slot count, only core's set grants 2/4/6 bonuses
HT Chips: chip layout now matches in-game arrangement (1/2/3 left, 4/5/6 right)
Feedback widget now includes battle report ID when submitting from report-linked pages
UD Sector Map — interactive grid with wasteland ownership, neutral cities, and round-by-round history
Battle Report: Hero Equipment modal — view exclusive skills, titan gear, runes, and stats
Great Offers Preview page — pricing breakdowns with real-cost JPY conversion
Easter 2026 event page with wishing well mechanics
Site-wide dark/light theme toggle and Google Translate on all pages
Battle plan page with squad assignments and position mapping
Feedback widget on all beta pages
Push notifications v2 with per-event reminders and alliance targeting
Battle Report Viewer — decode any PvP report with hero, decoration, and unit breakdowns
Decoration Index — 300+ decorations with levels, buffs, mutual exclusives, and suit sets
Base Skins Index — 287 castle skins with city skills and decorative effects
Heroes Database — 139 heroes with skills, stats, and type filters
UD Treasury Distribution system with fair allocation methodology
Calendar with custom events, tag filtering, and daily backup
Notification inbox and Armory Report with rune system
Server 2864 landing page with orbital navigation wheel
UD Calendar with seal stone schedule
Google Translate integration and language picker
Rockfield text guide and defense guide
